title: carex stolonifera subsp. alpina
intro: Carex stolonifera subsp. alpina is a subspecies of sedge plant, a type of grass-like plant, known for its distinctive growth habit and ability to thrive in alpine environments. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Carex stolonifera subsp. alpina are thought to include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using them in decoctions, although commercial production and standardization of these preparations are limited.
